header: S. S. da Grama (SP) / Pocos de Caldas (MG)

lesson.menu_desc: Asking for Directions -- Imperatives & Prepositions of Place -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 4 -- Street Smart -- Asking for Directions
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Tuca Dias | Aula 4 -- Street Smart

listenings.text: Hi Tuca, it's Mateo. You asked me how to reach the specialty coffee shop downtown, so here are the directions. From your hotel, turn right and walk straight ahead for two blocks until you reach the big avenue. At the second traffic light, cross at the crosswalk and keep walking on the same sidewalk. You will pass a small square on your left. The coffee shop is on the corner, right next to a bookstore and across from the old church. If you reach the roundabout, you have gone too far. The church is the easiest landmark, so just look for it. Honestly, I wish I could meet you there, but I'm stuck at work. Enjoy the coffee, and call me if you get lost.

Hey, it's Tuca. I finally found that famous coffee shop, and the walk was easier than I expected. I just followed Mateo's directions: I turned right, went straight ahead for two blocks, and crossed at the crosswalk by the big avenue. The little square was the perfect landmark. The shop sits right on the corner, next to a bookstore and opposite a beautiful old church. Yesterday I wasted a whole hour walking the wrong way, so next time I'll just ask a local right away. Anyway, the coffee here is incredible, very different from ours back at the farm. Come and visit, and I'll take you here myself.

extra_audio.text: Tuca leaves her hotel and realizes she does not know the way. She stops a friendly local and asks for directions. The local tells her to go straight ahead and turn at the traffic light. Tuca walks past the square and finds the corner. She arrives at the coffee shop, right next to the bookstore.
